from.title: Restoration Of Peter Three Times
from.body: # Threefold Restoration of Peter ## NT Text — John 21:15-19 By a charcoal fire Jesus asks three times 'do you love me' — undoing three denials and recommissioning Peter to shepherd. ## OT Antecedents - **2 Sam 12** — Nathan-David restoration. - **Ezek 34** — True shepherd. - **Jer 3:12-14** — Return, faithless one. ## Shared structure - Threefold question - Charcoal-fire setting - Recommission - F…

to.title: Three Denials Of Peter
to.body: # Three Denials of Peter ## NT Text — Matt 26:69-75; John 18:15-27 Peter denies Jesus three times before the cock crows, as foretold. The threefold structure will be answered by the threefold restoration in John 21. ## OT Antecedents - **2 Sam 12:13** — David's confession after threefold rebuke — pattern of fall and restoration. - **Zech 13:7** — Strike the shepherd and the sheep will be scattered…

from.title: Daniel 6 Lions Den
from.body: # Daniel in the Lions' Den → Sealed Tomb and Vindication ## OT Story — Daniel 6 Daniel prays openly; he is cast to the lions; the stone sealed with the king's signet. At dawn the king finds him alive; God shut the lions' mouths. The accusers are devoured. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 11:33: By faith they stopped the mouths of lions. - 2 Timothy 4:17: I was rescued from the lion's mouth. - Matthew 2…

to.title: Sealing Of Stone
to.body: # Sealing of the Stone ## NT Text — Matt 27:62-66 Pilate authorizes a guard and seal on the tomb. The precaution becomes evidence: theft cannot explain the empty tomb. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 6:17** — Sealed lions' den. - **Lam 3:53** — Stone over the pit. - **Job 14:13** — Hide me in Sheol. ## Shared structure - Stone over opening - Official seal - Guard posted - Confinement of righteous ## The…

from.title: Shipwreck On Malta
from.body: # Shipwreck on Malta ## NT Text — Acts 27-28 Paul survives shipwreck and viper-bite; the apostle's preservation echoes Jonah's storm and Genesis 3's serpent-defeat. ## OT Antecedents - **Jon 1** — Storm at sea, prophet aboard. - **Gen 3:15** — Heel and serpent — viper defeated. - **Ps 107:23-32** — Storm-saved travelers. ## Shared structure - Storm - Apostolic calm - Salvation of all aboard - Serp…

to.title: Paul Shipwreck Survival
to.body: # Paul's Shipwreck Survival ## What happens On the way to Rome, Paul's ship is caught in a fourteen-day storm and wrecked on Malta; an angel promises that all 276 souls will be saved, and they are. ## Typological significance Anti-Jonah: the prophet's presence on board, far from causing the storm, secures the salvation of all. Paul's testimony in the storm and the bread he breaks with sailors pref…

from.title: Garden Of Gethsemane As Eden Reversed
from.body: # Garden of Gethsemane as Eden Reversed ## NT Text — Matt 26:36-46; John 18:1 Jesus enters a garden to face the will of his Father. Where Adam in the garden chose his own will and brought death, the last Adam chooses the Father's will and accepts death to bring life. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2-3** — Eden — the garden of disobedience. - **Gen 3:17-19** — Toil and sweat as curse. - **2 Sam 15:23** …

to.title: Garden Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Garden Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 18-20; Rev 22 Eden, Song of Songs' enclosed garden, Gethsemane, the resurrection garden, the city-garden of Revelation 22 — gardens trace the arc of communion lost, recovered, consummated. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2-3** — Eden garden. - **Song 4:12-16** — Sealed garden of love. - **Isa 51:3** — Wilderness made like Eden. - **Ezek 36:35** — Land beco…

from.title: Beatitudes As New Torah
from.body: # Beatitudes as New Torah ## NT Text — Matt 5:3-12; Luke 6:20-26 The Beatitudes open the Sermon by pronouncing blessing on the unlikely — poor in spirit, mourners, meek, hungry for righteousness. They invert OT expectation while fulfilling its deepest grain. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 1** — Blessed is the man who walks not in counsel of the wicked. - **Isa 61:1-3** — Good news to the poor, comfort t…

to.title: Sermon On Mount New Sinai
to.body: # Sermon on the Mount as New Sinai ## NT Text — Matt 5-7 Jesus ascends a mountain, sits down, and authoritatively teaches his disciples. Matthew structures the scene to evoke Moses at Sinai — but the new lawgiver speaks not 'thus says the LORD' but 'I say to you.' ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 19-20** — Moses on Sinai delivers the Decalogue. - **Deut 30:11-14** — Word near you — internalized Torah an…

from.title: Isaiah 53 Suffering Servant
from.body: # Isaiah 53 Suffering Servant → Christ's Passion ## OT Story — Isaiah 52:13-53:12 The fourth and climactic Servant Song. The Servant is *"despised and rejected"* (53:3), *"pierced for our transgressions, crushed for our iniquities"* (53:5). He is silent before his accusers — *"like a lamb that is led to the slaughter, and like a sheep that before its shearers is silent, so he opened not his mouth"…

to.title: Suffering Servant
to.body: # Suffering Servant The figure of Isaiah's four Servant Songs (42, 49, 50, 52:13-53:12) — bearer of iniquity, pierced for transgression, vindicated after death. ## Key passages - Isaiah 42:1-9 — first song - Isaiah 49:1-13 — second song - Isaiah 50:4-11 — third song - Isaiah 52:13-53:12 — fourth song - Matthew 8:17; 12:18-21 — fulfillment citations - Acts 8:32-35 — Philip and the eunuch - 1 Peter …

from.title: Josiahs Reforms
from.body: # Josiah Finds the Book / Reform → Word-Rediscovered Renewal ## OT Story — 2 Kings 22-23 During temple repairs, the Book of the Law is found. Josiah hears it read, tears his clothes, and leads sweeping reform: idols destroyed, Passover restored. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 4:12: The word of God is living and active. - Acts 17:30-31: God commands all people everywhere to repent. - Ezra 8-Nehemiah 8…

to.title: Josiah Renewal Covenant
to.body: # Josiah Renewal Covenant ## What it is King Josiah's covenant renewal after the rediscovery of the Book of the Law in the temple, in which king and people stand and pledge to walk after the LORD and keep his commandments with all their heart. ## Key elements - Triggered by rediscovery of the law book - Public reading by the king - King standing by the pillar - Comprehensive cultic purge follows -…

from.title: Boaz Ruth
from.body: # Boaz and Ruth → Christ the Kinsman-Redeemer The book of Ruth is structured as an extended illustration of the *goel* (kinsman-redeemer) law in Leviticus 25. It is also the only OT book named after a Gentile, foreshadowing the inclusion of the nations in Christ. ## OT Story — Ruth 2-4 Naomi loses her husband and both sons in Moab. She returns to Bethlehem as a widow, with her **Moabite (Gentile)*…

to.title: Boaz Foot Of Cross Threshing
to.body: # Threshing Floor of Boaz → Approaching the Redeemer in Boldness ## OT Story — Ruth 3 At Naomi's instruction, Ruth comes by night to the threshing floor where Boaz sleeps. She lies at his feet and asks him to spread his wing over her. He responds with covenant kindness. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 4:16: Draw near with boldness to the throne of grace. - Romans 5:8: While we were yet sinners, Christ…

from.body: # 1 Peter 2: Living Stones ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:4-8 Coming to Christ as the living stone, you yourselves like living stones are being built into a spiritual house. from.title: dikaiosune
from.body: # dikaiosune (δικαιοσύνη) — righteousness ## Semantic field Righteousness, justice — both ethical conformity to a standard and (Pauline) the saving rectifying action of God. Carries forensic ("declared righteous") and relational ("rightly related") senses. ## Theological weight "The dikaiosune of God is revealed in the gospel" (Rom 1:17). God's saving righteousness justifies the ungodly through fa…

to.title: tsedeq
to.body: # tsedeq (צֶדֶק) — righteousness ## Semantic field Conformity to the right standard of relationship — covenantal rightness expressed in justice. Related form *tsedaqah* emphasizes righteous action. Often paired with mishpat (judgment). ## Theological weight God's tsedeq is His saving righteousness — He puts things right in covenant. Abraham's faith was reckoned as tsedaqah (Gen 15:6), grounding th…

from.body: # Hosea 11 / 'Out of Egypt I Called My Son' → Christ Recapitulates Israel ## OT Story — Hosea 11:1 'When Israel was a child, I loved him, and out of Egypt I called my son.' to.title: Israel As Firstborn
to.body: # Israel My Firstborn → Christ the Firstborn ## OT Story — Exodus 4:22-23 The LORD's word to Pharaoh: 'Israel is my firstborn son. Let my son go that he may serve me. from.title: True Vine
from.body: # True Vine Jesus' "I am" claim in John 15, recasting Israel's vine identity (Ps 80, Isa 5, Ezek 15) around himself. ## Key passages - Psalm 80:8-19 — Israel as the vine - Isaiah 5:1-7 — the song of the vineyard - Jeremiah 2:21 — "I planted you a choice vine" - Ezekiel 15; 17:5-10 — vine parables - John 15:1-8 — "I am the true vine" ## Cross-references - `[[../Persons/David]]` — Davidic vine in Ps…

to.title: True Vine Discourse
to.body: # The True Vine Discourse ## What happens Jesus declares 'I am the true vine, my Father is the vinedresser.' Branches abiding in Him bear much fruit; those that do not are pruned away and burned. ## Typological significance Israel as failed vine (Psalm 80, Isaiah 5) replaced by Christ as the true vine — believers as branches in Him. The vine metaphor reframes covenant union as organic indwelling. …

from.title: abba
from.body: # abba (אַבָּא) — father ## Semantic field Aramaic for "father" — an intimate, family address. Not as informal as "daddy" (older scholarship overstated this), but warmly personal. ## Theological weight Jesus addresses God as Abba in Gethsemane (Mark 14:36) — astonishing intimacy. By the Spirit of adoption we cry "Abba!" (Rom 8:15; Gal 4:6) — sharing in Jesus' own filial address. ## Septuagint mapp…

to.title: av
to.body: # av (אָב) — father ## Semantic field Father — biological, ancestral, or relational head. Applied to God especially in relation to Israel as son and to the Davidic king as son. ## Theological weight God's fatherhood structures the covenant — Israel as His firstborn, the king as His son. Jesus' intimate Father-language (Abba) deepens this into the heart of trinitarian revelation. ## Septuagint mapp…

from.title: Words From Cross Father Forgive
from.body: # Word from Cross: Father, Forgive Them ## NT Text — Luke 23:34 Jesus intercedes for his crucifiers, echoing the Servant who 'made intercession for transgressors.' ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:12** — Intercession for transgressors. - **Num 14:19-20** — Moses intercedes for Israel. - **Gen 50:17** — Joseph forgives brothers. ## Shared structure - Intercession amid suffering - Forgiveness offered - …

to.body: # Word from Cross: Into Your Hands I Commit ## NT Text — Luke 23:46 Jesus dies praying Psalm 31:5 — a Davidic prayer of trust. Stephen later echoes it. Death is recast as commendation. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 31:5** — Into your hand I commit my spirit. - **Eccl 12:7** — Spirit returns to God. - **Acts 7:59** — Stephen echoes this prayer. ## Shared structure - Spirit-commendation - Trust in Father…

The flood typifies the cleansing-and-deliverance of Christian baptism. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 7-8** — Noah's flood and ark. - **Gen 6:5-8** — Wickedness met by judgment. - **Ezek 14:14** — Noah, Daniel, and Job — paradigmatic righteous. ## Shared struct…

to.title: Romans 6 Baptized Into Death
to.body: # Romans 6: Baptized into His Death ## NT Text — Rom 6:1-11 Believers are baptized into Christ's death and raised to walk in newness of life. from.title: City Throughout Scripture
from.body: # City Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Heb 11:10; Rev 21 From Cain's first city to Babel, Sodom, Jerusalem, Babylon, and the New Jerusalem, cities embody human collective destiny. Babylon vs. Zion is the canon's tale of two cities. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 4:17** — Cain founds a city. - **Gen 11** — Babel. - **Ps 48** — Beautiful Zion. - **Isa 47** — Babylon doomed. ## Shared structure - Collec…

from.title: 1 John 2 Atoning Sacrifice
from.body: # 1 John 2: Atoning Sacrifice ## NT Text — 1 John 2:1-2 If anyone sins we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous. He is the propitiation for our sins — and for the whole world. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement propitiation. - **Isa 53:10** — Soul an offering for sin. - **Ps 32:1-2** — Blessed is he whose sin is covered. ## Shared structure - Advocate - Propit…

to.title: Day Of Atonement High Priest
to.body: # High Priest on the Day of Atonement → Christ's Greater Entry ## OT Story — Leviticus 16 Once a year the high priest, after washing and offering for his own sin, enters the most holy place with blood, sprinkles the mercy seat, and emerges to bless the people. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 9:6-12: Christ entered once for all, with his own blood, into the greater tent not made with hands. - Hebrews 4…

from.title: 1 Peter 5 Chief Shepherd
from.body: # 1 Peter 5: Chief Shepherd ## NT Text — 1 Pet 5:1-4 Elders shepherd the flock; when the Chief Shepherd appears, they will receive the unfading crown of glory. Davidic-shepherd typology gathered into Christ. ## OT Antecedents - **Ezek 34:23** — My servant David — one shepherd. - **Ps 23** — The LORD is my shepherd. - **Zech 11:7-17** — Two shepherd-staffs. ## Shared structure - Under-shepherds - C…

to.title: Hebrews 5 Order Of Melchizedek
to.body: # Hebrews 5: Order of Melchizedek ## NT Text — Heb 5:5-10 Christ does not glorify himself but is appointed by God as high priest after the order of Melchizedek. Psalm 110:4 is the controlling text. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 14:18-20** — Melchizedek priest-king greets Abraham. - **Ps 110:4** — Priest forever after the order of Melchizedek. - **Isa 53:12** — Servant's exaltation through suffering. #…

from.title: Garden Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Garden Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 18-20; Rev 22 Eden, Song of Songs' enclosed garden, Gethsemane, the resurrection garden, the city-garden of Revelation 22 — gardens trace the arc of communion lost, recovered, consummated. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2-3** — Eden garden. - **Song 4:12-16** — Sealed garden of love. - **Isa 51:3** — Wilderness made like Eden. - **Ezek 36:35** — Land beco…

to.title: Revelation 21 New Jerusalem Descending
to.body: # Revelation 21: New Jerusalem Descending ## NT Text — Rev 21:1-22:5 John sees the new heaven and new earth and the New Jerusalem descending from heaven as a bride adorned. God dwells with men. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 65:17-25** — New heavens and new earth. - **Ezek 40-48** — Vision of restored temple-city. - **Zech 8:3** — I will dwell in Zion. ## Shared structure - New cosmos - City descending…

from.title: Aeneas Healed
from.body: # Aeneas Healed ## What happens At Lydda, Peter says to a paralytic of eight years, 'Aeneas, Jesus Christ heals you; rise and make your bed.' He rises immediately, and all of Sharon turns to the Lord. ## Typological significance Apostolic healing performed explicitly in Jesus' name and crediting Him directly — the apostle is transparent to the ongoing work of the risen Christ. Triggers regional co…

to.title: Healing At Beautiful Gate
to.body: # Healing at the Beautiful Gate ## What happens Peter and John, going up to the temple, command a lame man at the Beautiful Gate to walk in the name of Jesus; he leaps and enters the temple praising God. ## Typological significance Isaiah 35's 'the lame shall leap' fulfilled at the very threshold of the temple — the kingdom invading the old sanctuary. Jesus' authority continues through His apostle…

from.title: Light Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Light Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 1; Rev 21 Genesis's first creative word is 'let there be light'; the prophets promise light to the nations; Christ is the Light of the World; the New Jerusalem needs no lamp. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 1:3** — Let there be light. - **Isa 9:2** — People in darkness have seen great light. - **Isa 60:1-3** — Arise, shine. ## Shared structure - Creation li…

to.title: Water Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Water Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 4; John 7; Rev 22 Creation's waters, flood, Red Sea, wilderness rocks, Jordan, the Samaritan well, baptism, the river of life — water marks judgment and life. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 1:2** — Spirit hovering over waters. - **Exod 14** — Red Sea passage. - **Ezek 47** — River from the temple. ## Shared structure - Chaos-water - Judgment-water - Delive…

from.title: 1 Peter 2 He Bore Our Sins
from.body: # 1 Peter 2: He Bore Our Sins ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:21-25 Christ left an example; he himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we might die to sin and live to righteousness. Isaiah 53 is paraphrased throughout. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:4-6** — He bore our griefs; by his wounds we are healed. - **Isa 53:7** — Silent like a lamb. - **Isa 53:11** — Bear their iniquities. ## Shared struct…

to.title: Suffering Servant Isaiah 53
to.body: # Suffering Servant of Isaiah 53 ## The oracle The Servant is despised, pierced for our transgressions, crushed for our iniquities; like a lamb led to slaughter, he bears the sin of many and, after suffering, sees light and is exalted. ## Date / context Fourth Servant Song in Isaiah's exilic comfort section, late 8th c. BC, addressed to a future people who confess their misjudgment of him. ## Fulf…

from.title: 1 John 1 Walk In Light
from.body: # 1 John 1: Walk in the Light ## NT Text — 1 John 1:5-10 God is light; in him is no darkness. If we walk in the light we have fellowship with one another, and the blood of Jesus cleanses us from all sin. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 27:1** — The LORD is my light. - **Isa 60:1** — Arise, shine, for your light has come. - **Mic 7:8** — Though I sit in darkness, the LORD will be my light. ## Shared struc…

to.title: 2 John Walk In Truth
to.body: # 2 John: Walking in Truth ## NT Text — 2 John 4-6 John rejoices that the elect lady's children are walking in truth, just as we were commanded. Truth-walking as covenant fidelity. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 86:11** — Teach me your way, O LORD, that I may walk in your truth. - **Isa 38:3** — I have walked before you in truth. - **Hos 4:1** — No truth, no faithfulness in the land. ## Shared structure…

from.title: Hebrews 1 Heir Of All Things
from.body: # Hebrews 1: Heir of All Things ## NT Text — Heb 1:1-4 God has spoken in these last days by his Son, whom he appointed heir of all things, through whom also he made the world. Christ as final word and inheritor. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 2:8** — Ask of me, and I will give you the nations. - **Prov 8:22-31** — Wisdom present at creation. - **Deut 32:8-9** — Inheritance allotted. ## Shared structure …

to.title: Psalm 8 Son Of Man
to.body: # Psalm 8 / Son of Man Crowned → Christ's Dominion Restored ## OT Story — Psalm 8 'What is man that you are mindful of him, the son of man that you care for him?' Made a little lower than the heavenly beings, crowned with glory, all things under his feet. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 2:5-9: Applied to Jesus — we see Jesus crowned with glory and honor. - 1 Corinthians 15:27: All things put in subjec…

from.title: Daniel 12 Resurrection
from.body: # Daniel 12 / Many Awake → Resurrection to Life or Shame ## OT Story — Daniel 12:1-3 'Many of those who sleep in the dust of the earth shall awake — some to everlasting life, some to shame and contempt. Those who are wise shall shine like the brightness of the firmament.' ## NT Fulfillment - John 5:28-29: All in the tombs will come out — to resurrection of life or judgment. - Matthew 13:43: The ri…

to.title: Daniel 6 Lions Den
to.body: # Daniel in the Lions' Den → Sealed Tomb and Vindication ## OT Story — Daniel 6 Daniel prays openly; he is cast to the lions; the stone sealed with the king's signet. At dawn the king finds him alive; God shut the lions' mouths. The accusers are devoured. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 11:33: By faith they stopped the mouths of lions. - 2 Timothy 4:17: I was rescued from the lion's mouth. - Matthew 2…

from.title: Ezekiel 37 Dry Bones
from.body: # Ezekiel 37 Dry Bones → Resurrection ## OT Story — Ezekiel 37:1-14 In exile, Ezekiel is set down by the Spirit *"in the middle of the valley; it was full of bones ... and behold, they were very dry"* (37:1-2). God asks: *"Son of man, can these bones live?"* Ezekiel answers: *"O Lord GOD, you know"* (37:3). God commands him to prophesy to the bones: *"O dry bones, hear the word of the LORD ... Beh…

to.title: Bones Of Joseph Carried Out
to.body: # Bones of Joseph Carried Out → Hope of Resurrection ## OT Story — Genesis 50:24-26 Dying in Egypt, Joseph commands: 'God will surely visit you; carry up my bones from here.' Moses takes the bones at the exodus; Joshua buries them in the land. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 11:22: By faith Joseph spoke of the exodus and gave instructions concerning his bones. - Ezekiel 37: Dry bones live by the Spiri…

from.title: Micah Ruler From Old
from.body: # Micah 5 / Ruler From Old → Eternal Christ ## OT Story — Micah 5:2-5 'His goings forth are from of old, from ancient of days… He shall stand and shepherd his flock… And he shall be their peace.' ## NT Fulfillment - John 1:1: In the beginning was the Word. - Hebrews 13:8: Jesus Christ the same yesterday, today, and forever. - Ephesians 2:14: He himself is our peace. ## Shared structure - Pre-exist…

to.title: Zephaniah King In Midst
to.body: # Zephaniah 3 / The King in Your Midst → Christ Dwelling, Singing Over Us ## OT Story — Zephaniah 3:14-17 'Sing aloud, O daughter of Zion! The King of Israel, the LORD, is in your midst… He will rejoice over you with gladness; he will quiet you by his love; he will exult over you with loud singing.' ## NT Fulfillment - John 1:14: The Word became flesh and dwelt among us. - Matthew 1:23: God with u…

from.title: Revelation 1 Son Of Man Vision
from.body: # Revelation 1: Son of Man Vision ## NT Text — Rev 1:12-18 John sees one 'like a son of man' among golden lampstands — eyes like fire, feet like burnished bronze, voice like many waters. Daniel 7 and 10 are gathered. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 7:13** — One like a son of man. - **Dan 10:5-6** — Man clothed in linen, eyes like fire. - **Ezek 1:7** — Feet like burnished bronze. ## Shared structure - A…

to.title: Revelation 12 Dragon Cast Down
to.body: # Revelation 12: Dragon Cast Down ## NT Text — Rev 12:7-12 Michael and his angels war against the dragon; the dragon is cast down. The serpent of Eden meets ultimate defeat through the Lamb's blood and the saints' testimony. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 3:14-15** — Serpent crushed by the seed. - **Isa 14:12-15** — Daystar cast down. - **Dan 12:1** — Michael, prince of Israel, arises. ## Shared struct…

from.title: Day Of Atonement High Priest
from.body: # High Priest on the Day of Atonement → Christ's Greater Entry ## OT Story — Leviticus 16 Once a year the high priest, after washing and offering for his own sin, enters the most holy place with blood, sprinkles the mercy seat, and emerges to bless the people. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 9:6-12: Christ entered once for all, with his own blood, into the greater tent not made with hands. - Hebrews 4…

to.title: Hebrews 13 Outside The Camp
to.body: # Hebrews 13: Outside the Camp ## NT Text — Heb 13:11-14 Jesus suffered outside the gate to sanctify the people through his own blood. Let us go to him outside the camp, bearing his reproach. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16:27** — Sin-offering carcasses burned outside the camp. - **Num 19:3** — Red heifer slain outside the camp. - **Exod 33:7** — Tent of meeting pitched outside the camp. ## Shared st…

from.title: Cup Of Cup Of Wrath
from.body: # Cup of the Lord's Wrath ## NT Text — Matt 26:39; Mark 14:36 Jesus prays that the 'cup' pass from him. OT 'cup' imagery is consistently the cup of God's wrath against sin. Christ drinks what his people deserved. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 51:17** — Cup of his wrath, the bowl of staggering. - **Jer 25:15-29** — Cup of wrath given to the nations. - **Ps 75:8** — Cup with foaming wine. ## Shared stru…

to.title: Psalm 69 Reproaches Fell On Me
to.body: # Psalm 69 / 'Zeal for Your House Has Consumed Me' → Christ's Passion ## OT Story — Psalm 69 The psalmist drowns in deep waters; reproach has broken his heart; they gave him vinegar to drink; zeal for God's house consumes him. ## NT Fulfillment - John 2:17: 'Zeal for your house will consume me' at the temple cleansing. - John 15:25: 'They hated me without cause.' - Romans 15:3: The reproaches of t…

from.title: Bethlehem Birthplace
from.body: # Bethlehem as Messianic Birthplace ## NT Text — Matt 2:1-6; Luke 2:1-7 Jesus is born in Bethlehem, fulfilling Micah's oracle that the ruler of Israel would come from this small town. The same place that produced David now produces David's greater Son. ## OT Antecedents - **Mic 5:2** — Out of Bethlehem comes the ruler whose origins are from of old. - **1 Sam 16:1-13** — David anointed in Bethlehem…

to.title: David Anointed Youngest
to.body: # David Anointed in Bethlehem → Christ Anointed ## OT Story — 1 Samuel 16:1-13 Samuel comes to Bethlehem. Jesse parades seven sons; God chooses none. David, the youngest, is summoned from the sheep. Samuel anoints him; the Spirit rushes upon him. ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 13:22: God raised David to be king, a man after his own heart. - Luke 4:18: The Spirit of the Lord is upon me; he has anointed m…

from.title: Isaiah 50 Third Servant Song
from.body: # Third Servant Song / I Gave My Back → Christ Set His Face ## OT Story — Isaiah 50:4-9 'I gave my back to those who strike, my cheeks to those who pull out the beard… I have set my face like a flint; I know I shall not be put to shame.' ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 9:51: He set his face to go to Jerusalem. - Matthew 26:67: They spat in his face and struck him. - Matthew 27:14: He gave no answer. ## S…

to.title: Suffering Servant Isaiah 53
to.body: # Suffering Servant of Isaiah 53 ## The oracle The Servant is despised, pierced for our transgressions, crushed for our iniquities; like a lamb led to slaughter, he bears the sin of many and, after suffering, sees light and is exalted. ## Date / context Fourth Servant Song in Isaiah's exilic comfort section, late 8th c. BC, addressed to a future people who confess their misjudgment of him. ## Fulf…

from.title: 1 Cor 10 Rock Was Christ
from.body: # 1 Cor 10: The Rock Was Christ ## NT Text — 1 Cor 10:1-13 Paul reads Israel's wilderness experiences typologically: cloud, sea, spiritual food and drink — 'and the Rock was Christ.' Sacrament-shaped warning. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 14** — Cloud and sea. - **Exod 17** — Water from the rock. - **Num 20** — Second rock-incident. ## Shared structure - Cloud/sea as baptism - Manna as spiritual food…

to.title: Crossing Red Sea Baptism
to.body: # Crossing the Red Sea → Baptism Into Moses, Baptism Into Christ ## OT Story — Exodus 14 Israel walks through the sea on dry ground; the waters return and drown Pharaoh's army. The old life of slavery is buried in the sea. ## NT Fulfillment - 1 Corinthians 10:1-2: 'All were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea.' - Romans 6:3-4: Baptized into Christ's death, raised to walk in newness. - …

from.title: Job Mediator Daysman
from.body: # Job's Cry for a Daysman → Christ Our Mediator ## OT Story — Job 9:32-35; 16:19-21 Job: 'There is no umpire between us… Even now my witness is in heaven; he who testifies for me is on high. Oh, that one might plead for a man with God!' He longs for a daysman to lay his hand on both. ## NT Fulfillment - 1 Timothy 2:5: One mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus. - Hebrews 7:25: He alway…

to.title: Job My Redeemer Lives
to.body: # Job / 'My Redeemer Lives' → Resurrection Hope ## OT Story — Job 19:25-27 'I know that my Redeemer lives, and at the last he will stand upon the earth. After my skin has been thus destroyed, yet in my flesh I shall see God.' ## NT Fulfillment - John 11:25: I am the resurrection and the life. - 1 Corinthians 15:20: Christ has been raised, the firstfruits. - Revelation 1:18: I died, and behold I am…

from.title: Boaz Ruth
from.body: # Boaz and Ruth → Christ the Kinsman-Redeemer The book of Ruth is structured as an extended illustration of the *goel* (kinsman-redeemer) law in Leviticus 25. It is also the only OT book named after a Gentile, foreshadowing the inclusion of the nations in Christ. ## OT Story — Ruth 2-4 Naomi loses her husband and both sons in Moab. She returns to Bethlehem as a widow, with her **Moabite (Gentile)*…

to.title: Melchizedek
to.body: # Melchizedek → Christ as Eternal King-Priest A typology developed at length in **Hebrews 7**, anchored on a single verse from Psalm 110 and a brief Genesis episode. The argument is structural: Melchizedek's *absence* of genealogy, his combination of *kingly and priestly* offices, and his appearance *before the Levitical priesthood existed* together prefigure Christ. ## OT Story — Genesis 14:17-20…

from.title: Pillar Of Cloud And Fire
from.body: # Pillar of Cloud and Fire → God Tabernacling With Us ## OT Story — Exodus 13:21-22; 40:34-38 The LORD goes before Israel as a pillar of cloud by day and fire by night. The pillar fills the finished tabernacle. ## NT Fulfillment - John 1:14: The Word became flesh and tabernacled among us; we beheld his glory. - Matthew 17:5: A bright cloud overshadows; 'this is my beloved Son.' - Revelation 21:23:…

to.title: Veil Of Tabernacle
to.body: # Veil of the Tabernacle Torn → New and Living Way ## OT Story — Exodus 26:31-33 A heavy veil with cherubim separates the holy place from the most holy. Only the high priest enters, once a year, with blood. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 27:51: At Christ's death the veil is torn from top to bottom. - Hebrews 10:19-22: We enter the holy places by the blood of Jesus, by a new and living way opened thro…

from.title: Darkness Over Land
from.body: # Darkness Over the Land ## NT Text — Matt 27:45 Darkness covers the land from noon to three. The cosmic sign echoes the ninth Egyptian plague and Amos's day-of-the-Lord. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 10:21-23** — Three days darkness over Egypt. - **Amos 8:9** — Sun goes down at noon. - **Joel 2:31** — Sun turned to darkness. ## Shared structure - Daylight extinguished - Cosmic mourning - Plague echo…

to.title: Veil Of Temple Torn
to.body: # Veil of the Temple Torn ## NT Text — Matt 27:51 At Christ's death the temple veil tears top to bottom. The barrier between holy and most-holy is rent. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 26:31-33** — Veil separating holy from most-holy. - **Lev 16:2** — Aaron enters only on Day of Atonement. - **Isa 25:7** — He will destroy the covering. ## Shared structure - Top-down tearing - Sanctuary access - End of …

from.title: Sirach Wisdom Personified
from.body: # Sirach 24 / Wisdom Pitches Her Tent → Word Tabernacled ## OT Story — Sirach 24 Wisdom says: 'I came forth from the mouth of the Most High… The Creator commanded, Pitch your tent in Jacob. So in Zion I was established.' ## NT Fulfillment - John 1:14: The Word became flesh and tabernacled among us. - Colossians 2:3: Treasures of wisdom hidden in Christ. - Proverbs 8: Wisdom personified. ## Shared …

to.title: Wisdom Calls At Gates
to.body: # Wisdom Calls at the Gates → Christ Pleads with the City ## OT Story — Proverbs 1:20-33; 8:1-11 Wisdom raises her voice in the streets, at the gates, in the public square: 'How long, simple ones, will you love simplicity?' She offers life; refusal brings calamity. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 23:37: How often I would have gathered you, but you would not. - Luke 13:34: O Jerusalem, Jerusalem. - Rev…

from.title: 1 Peter 1 Born Anew
from.body: # 1 Peter 1: Born Anew through Living Word ## NT Text — 1 Pet 1:22-25 Born again through the living and abiding word of God — Isaiah's 'grass withers, the word stands forever' cited as charter. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 40:6-8** — Grass withers, word stands forever. - **Ezek 36:26** — New heart and Spirit. - **Ps 119:25** — Give me life according to your word. ## Shared structure - New birth - Liv…

to.title: 1 Peter 2 Royal Priesthood
to.body: # 1 Peter 2: Royal Priesthood ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:9-10 You are a chosen race, royal priesthood, holy nation, people for his possession — Exodus 19:6 reapplied to the church. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 19:5-6** — Kingdom of priests, holy nation. - **Hos 1:9-10; 2:23** — Not-my-people now my people. - **Isa 43:21** — People formed for myself. ## Shared structure - Exodus identity titles - Hosea rev…

from.title: 1 Peter 1 Living Hope
from.body: # 1 Peter 1: Living Hope ## NT Text — 1 Pet 1:3-9 Peter blesses God for a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ. The inheritance is imperishable, undefiled, unfading, kept in heaven. ## OT Antecedents - **Num 18:20** — LORD as inheritance. - **Ps 16:5-6** — The LORD is my chosen portion. - **Dan 12:2-3** — Resurrection inheritance. ## Shared structure - New birth - Living hope - Imp…

to.title: 1 Peter 2 Royal Priesthood
to.body: # 1 Peter 2: Royal Priesthood ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:9-10 You are a chosen race, royal priesthood, holy nation, people for his possession — Exodus 19:6 reapplied to the church. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 19:5-6** — Kingdom of priests, holy nation. - **Hos 1:9-10; 2:23** — Not-my-people now my people. - **Isa 43:21** — People formed for myself. ## Shared structure - Exodus identity titles - Hosea rev…

from.title: Revelation 13 Mark 666
from.body: # Revelation 13: Mark and 666 ## NT Text — Rev 13:16-18 All are forced to receive a mark — number of a man, six hundred sixty-six. Counter-seal to the saints' marking; failure-of-seven trebled. ## OT Antecedents - **Ezek 9:4** — Mark on foreheads of the faithful. - **Dan 3:5-7** — Forced worship of image. - **1 Kgs 10:14** — Solomon's annual gold: 666 talents — kingdom-pride. ## Shared structure -…

to.title: Revelation 5 Scroll And Seals
to.body: # Revelation 5: Scroll and Seven Seals ## NT Text — Rev 5:1-7 A scroll sealed with seven seals — none worthy to open. The Lamb takes it. Daniel's sealed words and Ezekiel's scroll converge. ## OT Antecedents - **Ezek 2:9-3:3** — Scroll given to the prophet. - **Dan 12:4** — Seal the book until the time of the end. - **Isa 29:11** — Words of a sealed book. ## Shared structure - Sealed scroll - Sear…

from.title: Pauls Defense Before Felix
from.body: # Paul's Defense Before Felix ## NT Text — Acts 24 Paul defends himself before Felix, speaking of righteousness, self-control, and the coming judgment. Felix is alarmed and dismisses him. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 5** — Belshazzar trembles before the writing on the wall. - **Esth 6** — King unable to sleep before reversal. - **Ezek 33** — Watchman warning. ## Shared structure - Imprisoned witness …

to.title: Trial Before Caiaphas
to.body: # Trial Before Caiaphas ## NT Text — Matt 26:57-68 The Sanhedrin condemns Jesus when he identifies himself with Daniel 7's Son of Man coming on clouds and Psalm 110's enthroned Lord. The judges become the judged. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 7:13-14** — Son of Man on the clouds. - **Ps 110:1** — Sit at my right hand. - **Isa 53:7** — Silent like a lamb. ## Shared structure - False witnesses - Silent …

from.title: 1 John 2 Atoning Sacrifice
from.body: # 1 John 2: Atoning Sacrifice ## NT Text — 1 John 2:1-2 If anyone sins we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous. He is the propitiation for our sins — and for the whole world. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement propitiation. - **Isa 53:10** — Soul an offering for sin. - **Ps 32:1-2** — Blessed is he whose sin is covered. ## Shared structure - Advocate - Propit…

to.title: Hebrews 7 Forever Priest
to.body: # Hebrews 7: A Priest Forever ## NT Text — Heb 7:23-28 Christ holds his priesthood permanently because he continues forever. He is able to save to the uttermost because he always lives to intercede. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 110:4** — Priest forever. - **Lev 16** — Annual atonement — repeated. - **Num 25:13** — Phinehas's perpetual priesthood — partial type. ## Shared structure - Eternal priesthood…

from.title: 1 Cor 10 Rock Was Christ
from.body: # 1 Cor 10: The Rock Was Christ ## NT Text — 1 Cor 10:1-13 Paul reads Israel's wilderness experiences typologically: cloud, sea, spiritual food and drink — 'and the Rock was Christ.' Sacrament-shaped warning. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 14** — Cloud and sea. - **Exod 17** — Water from the rock. - **Num 20** — Second rock-incident. ## Shared structure - Cloud/sea as baptism - Manna as spiritual food…

to.title: 1 Peter 2 Living Stones
to.body: # 1 Peter 2: Living Stones ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:4-8 Coming to Christ as the living stone, you yourselves like living stones are being built into a spiritual house. Psalm 118 and Isaiah 28 quoted. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 28:16** — Cornerstone in Zion — quoted. - **Ps 118:22** — Stone the builders rejected — quoted. - **Dan 2:34** — Stone cut without hands. ## Shared structure - Cornerstone - Livi…

from.title: Mountain Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Mountain Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Heb 12; Matt 5; Rev 21 Mountains structure the canon: Eden's high garden, Moriah, Sinai, Zion, the Mount of Transfiguration, Mount of Olives, the heavenly Mount Zion. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 22** — Moriah. - **Exod 19** — Sinai. - **2 Sam 5:7** — Zion. - **Ezek 28:14** — Holy mountain of God. ## Shared structure - Ascent - Theophany - Covenant - Sacri…

to.title: Temptation On Mountain
to.body: # Temptation on the Mountain: Kingdoms Offered ## NT Text — Matt 4:8-10; Luke 4:5-8 Satan offers Jesus the kingdoms of the world for an act of worship; Jesus answers from Deuteronomy. The episode echoes Moses on Pisgah viewing the land, but here a usurper claims to grant it. ## OT Antecedents - **Deut 6:13** — Worship the LORD alone — Jesus' citation. - **Deut 34:1-4** — Moses sees the kingdoms fr…

from.title: Absalom Rebellion
from.body: # Absalom's Rebellion → Son Rebels Against Father; Cross Subverts ## OT Story — 2 Samuel 15-18 Absalom steals the people's hearts, drives David from Jerusalem. David crosses the Kidron weeping, ascending the Mount of Olives. Eventually Absalom dies hanging in a tree. ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 19:14: 'We do not want this man to reign over us.' - John 18:1: Jesus crossed the Kidron — same path as Dav…

to.title: Kiss Of Judas
to.body: # Kiss of Judas ## NT Text — Matt 26:47-50; Luke 22:47-48 Judas betrays Jesus with a kiss. The gesture inverts covenant intimacy into treachery, echoing Joab's kiss-and-kill of Amasa and the proverb that an enemy's kisses are deceitful. ## OT Antecedents - **2 Sam 20:9-10** — Joab kisses Amasa and stabs him. - **Ps 41:9** — My close friend who ate my bread lifted his heel — cited in John 13:18. - …

from.title: Tabernacle Pattern From Heaven
from.body: # Tabernacle Built to Heavenly Pattern → Heavenly Sanctuary ## OT Story — Exodus 25:9; 25:40 Moses is to build the tabernacle 'according to the pattern shown you on the mountain.' The earthly is a copy of the heavenly. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 8:5: They serve a copy and shadow of the heavenly things. - Hebrews 9:23-24: Christ entered the true sanctuary, of which the earthly is a copy. - Revelat…

to.title: Temple Dedication Glory
to.body: # Glory Fills the Temple → Glory Fills the Church ## OT Story — 1 Kings 8:10-13 When the priests bring the ark into the new temple, the cloud fills the house, so the priests cannot stand. AD 30, looking at the Second Temple from across the Kidron. ## Fulfillment AD 70 fall of Jerusalem fulfills the near-ho…

from.title: Revelation 5 Scroll And Seals
from.body: # Revelation 5: Scroll and Seven Seals ## NT Text — Rev 5:1-7 A scroll sealed with seven seals — none worthy to open. The Lamb takes it. Daniel's sealed words and Ezekiel's scroll converge. ## OT Antecedents - **Ezek 2:9-3:3** — Scroll given to the prophet. - **Dan 12:4** — Seal the book until the time of the end. - **Isa 29:11** — Words of a sealed book. ## Shared structure - Sealed scroll - Sear…

to.title: Sealing Of Stone
to.body: # Sealing of the Stone ## NT Text — Matt 27:62-66 Pilate authorizes a guard and seal on the tomb. The precaution becomes evidence: theft cannot explain the empty tomb. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 6:17** — Sealed lions' den. - **Lam 3:53** — Stone over the pit. - **Job 14:13** — Hide me in Sheol. ## Shared structure - Stone over opening - Official seal - Guard posted - Confinement of righteous ## The…

from.title: Hebrews 8 New Covenant Better
from.body: # Hebrews 8: A Better New Covenant ## NT Text — Heb 8:6-13 Christ's ministry is more excellent because it mediates a better covenant. The author quotes Jeremiah 31 in full — laws on hearts, knowledge of the Lord, sins remembered no more. ## OT Antecedents - **Jer 31:31-34** — Behold the days are coming — I will make a new covenant. - **Ezek 36:26-27** — New heart and Spirit. - **Deut 30:6** — Circ…

to.title: Veil Of Tabernacle
to.body: # Veil of the Tabernacle Torn → New and Living Way ## OT Story — Exodus 26:31-33 A heavy veil with cherubim separates the holy place from the most holy. Only the high priest enters, once a year, with blood. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 27:51: At Christ's death the veil is torn from top to bottom. - Hebrews 10:19-22: We enter the holy places by the blood of Jesus, by a new and living way opened thro…

from.title: Pauls Defense Before Felix
from.body: # Paul's Defense Before Felix ## NT Text — Acts 24 Paul defends himself before Felix, speaking of righteousness, self-control, and the coming judgment. Felix is alarmed and dismisses him. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 5** — Belshazzar trembles before the writing on the wall. - **Esth 6** — King unable to sleep before reversal. - **Ezek 33** — Watchman warning. ## Shared structure - Imprisoned witness …

to.title: Trial Before Pilate Innocent Verdict
to.body: # Trial Before Pilate: Innocent Verdict ## NT Text — John 18:28-19:16 Pilate repeatedly declares Jesus innocent yet hands him over. The pattern matches the Servant unjustly condemned and the Passover lamb inspected and found without blemish. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:8-9** — Cut off though no violence in him. - **Exod 12:5** — Lamb without blemish. - **Ps 2:1-2** — Rulers gather against the LOR…

from.title: Glory Filling Temple Solomon
from.body: # Glory Filling Solomon's Temple ## The appearance At the temple dedication, the cloud fills the house of YHWH so that the priests cannot stand to minister; fire descends and consumes the offerings, and the glory of YHWH fills the house. ## Mode / context Solomon's dedication, c. 957 BC; covenant-renewal liturgy. ## Significance The Sinai-tabernacle pattern reaches its architectural climax — YHWH …

to.title: Pillar Of Cloud And Fire
to.body: # Pillar of Cloud and Fire ## The appearance YHWH goes before Israel by day in a pillar of cloud, and by night in a pillar of fire, to lead them; the same pillar stands between Israel and the Egyptians at the sea. ## Mode / context Throughout the wilderness wanderings; visible, mobile, and protective. ## Significance Continuous divine presence guiding the people; ancestor of the shekhinah glory th…

from.body: # Rock Gives Water → "The Rock Was Christ" (1 Cor 10:4) ## OT Story — Exodus 17 & Numbers 20 **Exodus 17:1-7** — Israel camps at Rephidim. There is no water. The people quarrel with Moses and *"tested the LORD by saying, 'Is the LORD among us or not?'"* (17:7). God commands Moses: *"Behold, I will stand before you there on **the rock at Horeb**, and you shall **strike the rock**, and water shall c…

to.title: Stone Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Stone Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2; Matt 21:42 Jacob's pillow-stone at Bethel, the Sinai tablets, the rejected cornerstone, Daniel's stone cut without hands, the living stone of 1 Peter — stones structure the canon. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 28:18** — Jacob's pillar at Bethel. - **Exod 31:18** — Stone tablets. - **Ps 118:22** — Stone the builders rejected. - **Dan 2:34-35** — Stone …

from.title: Melchizedek
from.body: # Melchizedek → Christ as Eternal King-Priest A typology developed at length in **Hebrews 7**, anchored on a single verse from Psalm 110 and a brief Genesis episode. The argument is structural: Melchizedek's *absence* of genealogy, his combination of *kingly and priestly* offices, and his appearance *before the Levitical priesthood existed* together prefigure Christ. ## OT Story — Genesis 14:17-20…

Now, lest he reach out his hand and take also of the tree of life and eat, and live forever..."* (Gen 3:22). He drives the man out, and *"at the east of the garden of Eden he placed the **cherubim** and a **flaming sword** that tur…

from.body: # Revelation 14: Lamb on Mount Zion ## NT Text — Rev 14:1-5 The Lamb stands on Mount Zion with 144,000 bearing his Father's name on their foreheads. Psalm 2's enthronement on Zion fulfilled. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 2:6** — I have set my king on Zion, my holy hill. - **Joel 2:32** — Mount Zion shall be refuge. - **Obad 17** — On Mount Zion there shall be deliverance. ## Shared structure - Lamb ent…

to.title: Revelation 18 Babylon Fallen
to.body: # Revelation 18: Babylon Fallen ## NT Text — Rev 18 Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great. Merchants and kings lament her destruction; the saints rejoice. Jeremiah and Ezekiel laments echoed. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 21:9** — Fallen, fallen is Babylon. - **Jer 51** — Detailed Babylon-doom. - **Ezek 27** — Lament over Tyre. ## Shared structure - Fallen-fallen formula - Merchant lament - Saints rejoi…

to.title: Romans 6 Baptized Into Death
to.body: # Romans 6: Baptized into His Death ## NT Text — Rom 6:1-11 Believers are baptized into Christ's death and raised to walk in newness of life. Baptism is union with the Death-and-Resurrection of Christ. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 14** — Red Sea passage as death/resurrection of Israel. - **Josh 3** — Jordan crossing into the land. - **Ezek 36:25-27** — Sprinkled with clean water; new spirit. ## Shar…

from.title: Doubting Thomas
from.body: # Doubting Thomas ## NT Text — John 20:24-29 Thomas demands to touch the wounds and confesses 'my Lord and my God.' Highest Johannine confession follows deepest doubt. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 35:23** — My God and my Lord. - **Zech 12:10** — Look on him whom they pierced. - **Isa 53:5** — By his wounds we are healed. ## Shared structure - Demand for evidence - Risen body encountered - Wound-venera…

to.title: Empty Tomb
to.body: # Empty Tomb ## NT Text — Matt 28:6; John 20:6-7 The tomb is empty; grave-clothes remain. The body is raised, not stolen. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 16:10** — You will not abandon my soul to Sheol. - **Hos 6:2** — Third day he will raise us. - **Isa 53:10-11** — He shall see his offspring. ## Shared structure - Tomb opened - Linens left - No body - Angelic announcement ## Theological motif Three-day…

from.title: 1 Cor 5 Christ Our Passover
from.body: # 1 Cor 5: Christ Our Passover ## NT Text — 1 Cor 5:7-8 Paul calls Christ 'our Passover lamb, sacrificed for us,' and exhorts cleansing of old leaven. Passover and Unleavened Bread become ethical patterns. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12** — Passover ordinance. - **Exod 13:6-7** — Unleavened bread for seven days. - **Deut 16:1-8** — Passover-Unleavened Bread combined. ## Shared structure - Lamb slai…

to.title: Hebrews 13 Outside The Camp
to.body: # Hebrews 13: Outside the Camp ## NT Text — Heb 13:11-14 Jesus suffered outside the gate to sanctify the people through his own blood. Let us go to him outside the camp, bearing his reproach. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16:27** — Sin-offering carcasses burned outside the camp. - **Num 19:3** — Red heifer slain outside the camp. - **Exod 33:7** — Tent of meeting pitched outside the camp. ## Shared st…

from.title: Bride Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Bride Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Eph 5; Rev 19, 21; John 3:29 From Eve to the New Jerusalem, the bride is a pattern of covenant love. Israel as bride, faithless and beloved; the church as bride; the city as bride. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2:21-24** — Eve formed from Adam's side. - **Hos 1-3** — YHWH's bride. - **Isa 62:5** — Bridegroom rejoices over bride. - **Ezek 16** — Faithless bride…

to.title: Water Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Water Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 4; John 7; Rev 22 Creation's waters, flood, Red Sea, wilderness rocks, Jordan, the Samaritan well, baptism, the river of life — water marks judgment and life. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 1:2** — Spirit hovering over waters. - **Exod 14** — Red Sea passage. - **Ezek 47** — River from the temple. ## Shared structure - Chaos-water - Judgment-water - Delive…

from.title: Forty Days In Wilderness
from.body: # Forty Days in the Wilderness ## NT Text — Matt 4:1-2; Mark 1:12-13; Luke 4:1-2 Jesus fasts forty days in the wilderness, recapitulating Israel's forty years, Moses' two forty-day fasts on Sinai, and Elijah's forty-day journey to Horeb. Where Israel failed, the Son succeeds. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 24:18; 34:28** — Moses forty days on Sinai. - **Num 14:33-34** — Israel forty years in the wilde…

to.title: Road To Emmaus
to.body: # Road to Emmaus ## NT Text — Luke 24:13-32 The risen Christ walks with two disciples and opens 'Moses and all the prophets' to show the necessity of messianic suffering. ## OT Antecedents - **Deut 18:15** — Prophet like Moses. - **Isa 53** — Suffering Servant. - **Ps 22; Ps 16** — Suffering and resurrection psalms. ## Shared structure - Walking journey - Stranger reveals Scripture - Hearts burnin…

from.title: Hagar Sarah Two Covenants
from.body: # Hagar & Sarah → Two Covenants (Paul's Allegory) ## OT Story — Genesis 16, 21 God promises Abraham a son. Years pass. Sarah, impatient, gives her Egyptian slave Hagar to Abraham as a surrogate (Gen 16:2). Hagar conceives Ishmael — *"a wild donkey of a man"* (16:12). Conflict immediately follows: Hagar despises Sarah; Sarah mistreats Hagar; Hagar flees and is met by the Angel of the LORD by a spri…

to.body: # Rebekah, Bride for Isaac → The Spirit Drawing the Bride of Christ ## OT Story — Genesis 24 Abraham is old. He commissions his **unnamed servant** (traditionally Eliezer of Damascus, Gen 15:2): *"Put your hand under my thigh, that I may make you swear by the LORD ... that you will not take a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites ... but will go to my country and to my kindred, and …

from.body: # Psalm 16 / 'You Will Not Let Your Holy One See Decay' → Resurrection Predicted ## OT Story — Psalm 16 David sings: 'I have set the LORD always before me… you will not abandon my soul to Sheol or let your holy one see corruption. You will show me the path of life.' ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 2:25-32: Peter — David died and was buried; he prophesied Christ's resurrection. - Acts 13:35-37: Paul — the…

to.body: # Psalm 40 / 'A Body You Have Prepared' → Incarnation for Sacrifice ## OT Story — Psalm 40:6-8 'Sacrifice and offering you did not desire, but a body you have prepared for me… Behold, I have come to do your will, O God.' The psalmist offers obedience above ritual. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 10:5-10: Applied directly to Christ entering the world. - Philippians 2:7-8: He emptied himself, obedient t…

from.body: # Centurion's Confession ## NT Text — Matt 27:54; Mark 15:39 A Gentile centurion confesses Jesus as 'Son of God.' The first post-cross confession comes from outside Israel. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 2:7-8** — Son of God to whom nations given. - **Isa 49:6** — Light to nations. - **Josh 2** — Rahab confesses Israel's God. ## Shared structure - Gentile witness - Sonship confession - First post-cross …

to.body: # Word from Cross: Father, Forgive Them ## NT Text — Luke 23:34 Jesus intercedes for his crucifiers, echoing the Servant who 'made intercession for transgressors.' ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:12** — Intercession for transgressors. - **Num 14:19-20** — Moses intercedes for Israel. - **Gen 50:17** — Joseph forgives brothers. ## Shared structure - Intercession amid suffering - Forgiveness offered - …

from.title: Isaiah 53 Suffering Servant
from.body: # Isaiah 53 Suffering Servant → Christ's Passion ## OT Story — Isaiah 52:13-53:12 The fourth and climactic Servant Song. The Servant is *"despised and rejected"* (53:3), *"pierced for our transgressions, crushed for our iniquities"* (53:5). He is silent before his accusers — *"like a lamb that is led to the slaughter, and like a sheep that before its shearers is silent, so he opened not his mouth"…

from.body: # Revelation 20: The Millennium ## NT Text — Rev 20:1-6 The dragon is bound; the saints reign with Christ a thousand years. to.body: # Revelation 7: Great Multitude ## NT Text — Rev 7:9-17 A great multitude no one could number, from every nation, tribe, people, language, standing before the throne and the Lamb. Abrahamic promise fulfilled. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 12:3** — All families blessed. - **Gen 22:17** — Stars and sand-multitude. - **Isa 49:6** — Light to the nations. ## Shared structure - Innumerable multitude - Diver…

from.body: # Light Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 1; Rev 21 Genesis's first creative word is 'let there be light'; the prophets promise light to the nations; Christ is the Light of the World; the New Jerusalem needs no lamp. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 1:3** — Let there be light. - **Isa 9:2** — People in darkness have seen great light. - **Isa 60:1-3** — Arise, shine. ## Shared structure - Creation li…

to.body: # Wine Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 2; Matt 26 Noah's vineyard, Melchizedek's wine, Passover cups, Isaiah's wine-feast, Cana's water-into-wine, the cup of the new covenant, the cup of wrath — wine threads the canon. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 9:20** — Noah's vineyard. - **Gen 14:18** — Melchizedek's bread and wine. - **Isa 25:6** — Aged wine on the mountain. - **Jer 25:15** — Cup of wrath…

from.body: # David Anointed in Bethlehem → Christ Anointed ## OT Story — 1 Samuel 16:1-13 Samuel comes to Bethlehem. Jesse parades seven sons; God chooses none. Samuel anoints him; the Spirit rushes upon him. ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 13:22: God raised David to be king, a man after his own heart. - Luke 4:18: The Spirit of the Lord is upon me; he has anointed m…

from.title: Covenant Of Peace
from.body: # Covenant of Peace ## What it is A prophetic-priestly covenant promising lasting shalom — security, fertility, and the abiding presence of God among his people — first given to Phinehas and later promised through Isaiah and Ezekiel. ## Key elements - Lasting shalom and security - Removal of wild beasts and violence - Fertility of the land - God's sanctuary in the midst forever - Linked to a David…

to.title: Everlasting Covenant

from.body: # Isaiah 61 / 'The Spirit of the Lord Is Upon Me' → Jubilee Christology ## OT Story — Isaiah 61:1-3 'The Spirit of the Lord GOD is upon me, because the LORD has anointed me to bring good news to the poor; to proclaim liberty to captives, the year of the LORD's favor.' ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 4:16-21: Jesus reads it in the Nazareth synagogue: 'Today this Scripture is fulfilled.' - Matthew 5:3-4: B…

to.title: Joel Spirit Poured Out
to.body: # Joel 2 / Spirit Poured on All Flesh → Pentecost ## OT Story — Joel 2:28-32 'I will pour out my Spirit on all flesh; your sons and daughters shall prophesy, your old men shall dream dreams… Everyone who calls on the name of the LORD shall be saved.' ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 2:16-21: Peter quotes Joel in full at Pentecost. - Romans 10:13: Everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved. -…

from.title: Daniel 6 Lions Den
from.body: # Daniel in the Lions' Den → Sealed Tomb and Vindication ## OT Story — Daniel 6 Daniel prays openly; he is cast to the lions; the stone sealed with the king's signet. At dawn the king finds him alive; God shut the lions' mouths. The accusers are devoured. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 11:33: By faith they stopped the mouths of lions. - 2 Timothy 4:17: I was rescued from the lion's mouth. - Matthew 2…

to.title: Samson Solo Victory In Death
to.body: # Samson's Death Brings Greater Victory → Christ Triumphs by Dying ## OT Story — Judges 16:23-31 Blinded, mocked, between two pillars, Samson prays once more. He pushes; the temple collapses; he kills more in his death than in his life. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 2:14-15: Through death he destroyed the one who has the power of death. - Colossians 2:15: He disarmed the powers, triumphing over them…

from.title: Olivet Discourse
from.body: # Olivet Discourse ## The oracle On the Mount of Olives, Jesus answers about the temple's destruction and his coming: wars, false messiahs, persecutions, abomination of desolation, tribulation, cosmic signs, and the Son of Man coming on the clouds. ## Date / context Holy Week, c. AD 30, looking at the Second Temple from across the Kidron. ## Fulfillment AD 70 fall of Jerusalem fulfills the near-ho…

to.title: Coming On Clouds
to.body: # Coming on the Clouds ## The appearance The Son of Man comes on the clouds of heaven with power and great glory; every eye will see him, and those who pierced him will mourn. ## Mode / context Promised at the ascension (Acts 1:11) and across NT eschatology. ## Significance The eschatological theophany — visible, universal, vindicating, judging. ## Connects to `[[../Titles/Son-Of-Man]]` ## Embeddi…

from.title: Shepherd Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Shepherd Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 10; Heb 13:20 Abel, Jacob, Moses, David — all shepherds. YHWH as Shepherd in Psalm 23 and Ezekiel 34. Christ as Good Shepherd who lays down his life. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 23** — The LORD is my shepherd. - **Ezek 34** — Faithless shepherds vs. true. - **Zech 13:7** — Strike the shepherd. ## Shared structure - Pastoral vocation - Sheep-guidance -…

to.title: Stone Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Stone Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2; Matt 21:42 Jacob's pillow-stone at Bethel, the Sinai tablets, the rejected cornerstone, Daniel's stone cut without hands, the living stone of 1 Peter — stones structure the canon. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 28:18** — Jacob's pillar at Bethel. - **Exod 31:18** — Stone tablets. - **Ps 118:22** — Stone the builders rejected. - **Dan 2:34-35** — Stone …

from.title: 2 Cor 5 Became Sin For Us
from.body: # 2 Cor 5:21: Became Sin for Us ## NT Text — 2 Cor 5:21 He who knew no sin was made sin for us, that we might become the righteousness of God in him. The most compressed statement of the great exchange. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16:21-22** — Scapegoat bearing iniquity. - **Isa 53:6** — LORD laid on him the iniquity of us all. - **Num 19** — Cleansing through death of clean offering. ## Shared stru…

to.title: Adam Christ Two Heads Romans 5
to.body: # Adam and Christ as Two Heads (Rom 5) ## NT Text — Rom 5:12-21 Paul reads humanity as represented by two federal heads: Adam, by whom sin and death entered, and Christ, by whom righteousness and life come. Two acts, two reigns, two destinies. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2-3** — Adam's covenant headship and fall. - **Ps 51:5** — Inherited sinfulness. - **Hos 6:7** — Like Adam, they transgressed the …

from.title: Isaiah 53 Suffering Servant
from.body: # Isaiah 53 Suffering Servant → Christ's Passion ## OT Story — Isaiah 52:13-53:12 The fourth and climactic Servant Song. The Servant is *"despised and rejected"* (53:3), *"pierced for our transgressions, crushed for our iniquities"* (53:5). He is silent before his accusers — *"like a lamb that is led to the slaughter, and like a sheep that before its shearers is silent, so he opened not his mouth"…

to.title: Cup Of Cup Of Wrath
to.body: # Cup of the Lord's Wrath ## NT Text — Matt 26:39; Mark 14:36 Jesus prays that the 'cup' pass from him. OT 'cup' imagery is consistently the cup of God's wrath against sin. Christ drinks what his people deserved. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 51:17** — Cup of his wrath, the bowl of staggering. - **Jer 25:15-29** — Cup of wrath given to the nations. - **Ps 75:8** — Cup with foaming wine. ## Shared stru…

from.title: Rock
from.body: # Symbol: Rock / Stone A rich symbol with multiple sub-patterns: **rock as God's stable presence**, **rock that gives water**, **stone the builders rejected**, **cornerstone**. ## Hebrew - **צוּר** (*tsur*) — rock as fortress, refuge. "The LORD is my rock" (Ps 18:2). - **סֶלַע** (*sela*) — cliff, crag. - **אֶבֶן** (*even*) — stone, often building material. ## Greek - **πέτρα** (*petra*) — bedrock,…

to.title: 1 Cor 10 Rock Was Christ
to.body: # 1 Cor 10: The Rock Was Christ ## NT Text — 1 Cor 10:1-13 Paul reads Israel's wilderness experiences typologically: cloud, sea, spiritual food and drink — 'and the Rock was Christ.' Sacrament-shaped warning. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 14** — Cloud and sea. - **Exod 17** — Water from the rock. - **Num 20** — Second rock-incident. ## Shared structure - Cloud/sea as baptism - Manna as spiritual food…

from.title: 2 Cor 5 Reconciliation Through Christ
from.body: # 2 Cor 5: Reconciliation through Christ ## NT Text — 2 Cor 5:18-21 God was in Christ reconciling the world to himself, not counting trespasses. He made him to be sin for us — the great exchange. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement bearing of iniquity. - **Isa 53:5-6** — Iniquity of us all laid on him. - **Hos 6:1** — He has torn that he may heal — reconciliation pattern. ## Shared s…

to.title: Galatians 3 Curse Of Law
to.body: # Galatians 3: Curse of the Law ## NT Text — Gal 3:10-14 Christ became a curse for us, hanging on a tree — fulfilling Deut 21:23 and Deut 27:26. The curse of the law is borne so that Abrahamic blessing flows to the nations. ## OT Antecedents - **Deut 21:23** — Cursed is everyone hanged on a tree. - **Deut 27:26** — Cursed is anyone who does not uphold this law. - **Gen 12:3** — Abrahamic blessing …

from.title: Last Supper As Passover
from.body: # Last Supper as Passover ## NT Text — Matt 26:17-30; Mark 14:12-26; Luke 22:7-23 The Last Supper is celebrated as a Passover meal, with Jesus reinterpreting bread and cup as his body and blood. The Exodus typology comes to its fulfillment: the true Passover Lamb is at table. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12** — The Passover meal in Egypt. - **Exod 24:8** — Blood of the covenant sprinkled on the peop…

to.title: Trial Before Pilate Innocent Verdict
to.body: # Trial Before Pilate: Innocent Verdict ## NT Text — John 18:28-19:16 Pilate repeatedly declares Jesus innocent yet hands him over. The pattern matches the Servant unjustly condemned and the Passover lamb inspected and found without blemish. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:8-9** — Cut off though no violence in him. - **Exod 12:5** — Lamb without blemish. - **Ps 2:1-2** — Rulers gather against the LOR…

from.title: Elijahs Mantle
from.body: # Elijah's Mantle / Ascension → Ascension and the Spirit ## OT Story — 2 Kings 2 Elijah is taken up by a chariot of fire. His mantle falls to Elisha, who receives a double portion of the spirit and parts the Jordan. ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 1:9-11: Jesus is taken up; a cloud receives him. - Acts 2:33: Having received the Spirit, he poured him out. - Luke 9:30-31: Elijah and Moses speak with Jesus …

to.title: Ezekiel 10 Glory Departs
to.body: # Glory Departs the Temple → Christ Departs the Temple ## OT Story — Ezekiel 10-11 The glory of the LORD lifts from the cherubim, moves to the threshold, and finally to the mountain east of the city. The temple is left desolate before destruction. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 23:38-24:2: 'Your house is left to you desolate'; not one stone left. - Luke 21:5-6: Foretelling the temple's fall. - Mark 1…

from.title: Hebrews 12 Mount Zion Not Sinai
from.body: # Hebrews 12: Mount Zion, Not Sinai ## NT Text — Heb 12:18-24 You have not come to Sinai with its terror but to Mount Zion, to the heavenly Jerusalem, to angels, to the assembly of the firstborn, to Jesus the mediator. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 19** — Sinai theophany of terror. - **Ps 48** — Zion's beauty. - **Isa 60** — Zion glorified. ## Shared structure - Sinai/terror contrast - Zion/grace des…

to.title: Twelve Apostles Echo Twelve Tribes
to.body: # Twelve Apostles Echo Twelve Tribes ## NT Text — Matt 19:28; Rev 21:14 Jesus promises the Twelve will sit on twelve thrones judging the twelve tribes; Revelation 21 names the foundations of the New Jerusalem after them. Tribal Israel and apostolic Israel meet in the heavenly city. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 49:28** — Twelve tribes of Israel. - **Exod 28:21** — Twelve stones on the high priest's br…

from.title: Forty Days In Wilderness
from.body: # Forty Days in the Wilderness ## NT Text — Matt 4:1-2; Mark 1:12-13; Luke 4:1-2 Jesus fasts forty days in the wilderness, recapitulating Israel's forty years, Moses' two forty-day fasts on Sinai, and Elijah's forty-day journey to Horeb. Where Israel failed, the Son succeeds. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 24:18; 34:28** — Moses forty days on Sinai. - **Num 14:33-34** — Israel forty years in the wilde…

to.title: Out Of Egypt I Called My Son
to.body: # Out of Egypt I Called My Son ## NT Text — Matt 2:15 Matthew applies Hosea 11:1 to Jesus' return from Egypt. The verse originally describes Israel's exodus; Matthew reads Israel as a type whose pattern is fulfilled in the Son. ## OT Antecedents - **Hos 11:1** — When Israel was a child I loved him, and out of Egypt I called my son. - **Exod 4:22-23** — Israel is my firstborn son. - **Num 24:8** — …

from.title: Crucifixion Between Two Thieves
from.body: # Crucifixion Between Two Thieves ## NT Text — Luke 23:32-43 Jesus is crucified between two criminals, fulfilling Isaiah's 'numbered with the transgressors.' One mocks, one believes — a microcosm of human response. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:12** — Numbered with the transgressors. - **Ps 22:16** — Pierce my hands and feet. - **Zech 13:7** — Strike the shepherd. ## Shared structure - Three crosse…

to.title: Cup Of Cup Of Wrath
to.body: # Cup of the Lord's Wrath ## NT Text — Matt 26:39; Mark 14:36 Jesus prays that the 'cup' pass from him. OT 'cup' imagery is consistently the cup of God's wrath against sin. Christ drinks what his people deserved. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 51:17** — Cup of his wrath, the bowl of staggering. - **Jer 25:15-29** — Cup of wrath given to the nations. - **Ps 75:8** — Cup with foaming wine. ## Shared stru…

from.title: Psalm 118 Hosanna
from.body: # Psalm 118 / Hosanna → Triumphal Entry ## OT Story — Psalm 118:25-26 'Save us, we pray, O LORD! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the LORD!' Sung at pilgrimage feasts as Israel ascends to Jerusalem. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 21:9: 'Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessed is he who comes…' - John 12:13: Palm branches and the same cry. - Matthew 23:39: 'You will not see me again until you say…'…

to.title: Psalm 23 Shepherd
to.body: # Psalm 23 / The LORD My Shepherd → Christ the Good Shepherd ## OT Story — Psalm 23 'The LORD is my shepherd, I shall not want… he restores my soul… valley of the shadow of death… your rod and staff comfort me… you prepare a table before me.' ## NT Fulfillment - John 10:11: I am the good shepherd; the good shepherd lays down his life. - Revelation 7:17: The Lamb at the center of the throne will sh…

from.title: Battle Of Armageddon
from.body: # Battle of Armageddon ## The oracle The nations gather against Jerusalem (Zechariah) or against the people of God on the mountains of Israel (Ezekiel's Gog/Magog); Revelation names the gathering place "Har-Magedon" and depicts the rider on the white horse defeating the kings of the earth. ## Date / context Multiple prophetic streams converging in apocalyptic literature. ## Fulfillment Awaits esch…

to.title: New Jerusalem Descending
to.body: # New Jerusalem Descending ## The oracle John sees the holy city, New Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, prepared as a bride; God dwells with humanity, every tear wiped away, no more death, mourning, or pain. ## Date / context Climax of Revelation; Ezekiel's temple vision is a major OT antecedent. ## Fulfillment Awaits consummation; foretasted in the church as the city set on a hill. #…
